data.title: L'Air Liquide S.A. reports Q3 sales
data.description:
• L'Air Liquide S.A. press release (OTCPK:AIQUY): Q3 Revenue of $6.76B (-0.7% Y/Y).
  • Gas & Services revenue in the 3rd quarter of 2024 reached 6,445 million euros, up by +3.6% on a comparable basis (including a contribution of +2.0% from Argentina).
  • Global Markets & Technologies posted a -4.6% decrease in revenue on a comparable basis to 207 million euros.
  • Industrial and financial investment decisions saw a record level of 1.4 billion euros in the 3rd quarter of 2024, thus exceeding 3.0 billion euros since the beginning of the year.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Deutsche Bank Q3 revenues up 5% Y/Y. reaffirms financial targets
Husqvarna AB (publ) reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Husqvarna AB (publ) press release (OTCPK:HSQVY): Q3 GAAP EPS of SEK -0.27 (0.22) 
  • Net sales decreased by 7% to SEK 9,739m (10,512). Changes in exchange rates impacted by -3%. Organic sales decreased by 4%.

data.title: Nidec reports six months results
data.description:
• Nidec press release (OTCPK:NJDCY): Q2 GAAP EPS of ¥65.76.
  • Revenue of Y1.29M (+11.2% Y/Y).
  • First half operating profit increased 4.9% Y/Y to ¥121.0 billion, marking a record high.
  • Quarterly operating profit stood at a record high.

data.title: European markets move higher amid corporate earnings flurry
data.description:
London (UKX) +0.09%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.15%

France (CAC:IND) +0.12%

The consumer confidence index in Denmark declined to -8.9 in October.

Industrial production in Lithuania rose 2.3% Y/Y in September.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) moved 0.26% higher, as corporate earnings roll out. Major European firms like AkzoNobel, Heineken, Roche, Swedbank and Volvo will report their latest results today.

On the corporate front, Deutsche Bank (DB) stock declined even though Germany’s largest lender swung back into profit in Q3 after a loss in Q2.

Coming up in the session: A lighter day on the economic calendar.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 2 basis points to 4.23%.

Germany's 10-year yield was down 1 basis point to 2.31%.

UK's 10-year yield was up 4 basis points to 4.18%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Arm to cancel chip design license deal with Qualcomm over contract dispute - report
data.description:
Arm Holdings (NASDAQ:ARM) is scrapping an architectural license deal that allows Qualcomm (NASDAQ:QCOM) to use its intellectual property to design chips, according to a document viewed by Bloomberg.

The British chip designer has given Qualcomm (QCOM), its longtime partner and one of its biggest customers, a mandated 60-day cancellation notice of the agreement.

The move escalates the legal feud between the chip firms, which began when Arm (ARM), majority-owned by SoftBank (OTCPK:SFTBY) sued Qualcomm (QCOM) in 2022 for breach of contract and trademark infringement.

According to Arm's (ARM) lawsuit, Qualcomm (QCOM) failed to renegotiate the terms of the license deal after its acquisition of Nuvia, a chipmaking startup that was also an Arm licensee. But Qualcomm argued that the existing license deal covers Nuvia's activities.

Qualcomm (QCOM) this week said it plans to bring Nuvia's design Oryon to its Snapdragon chips for smartphones. Arm (ARM) claims this is a breach of their license deal and Nuvia designs created before its acquisition can't be transferred to Qualcomm without permission.

If Arm (ARM) moves ahead with canceling the license deal, Qualcomm (QCOM) may have to stop selling processors used in a majority of Android smartphones or face damages. It would also be prevented from using Arm's IP for its designs.

A Qualcomm (QCOM) spokesperson told Bloomberg that Arm (ARM) was trying to "strong-arm a longtime partner," calling its claim for termination "completely baseless."

data.title: Lloyds Banking reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Lloyds Banking press release (NYSE:LYG): Q3 GAAP EPS of 1.9p
  • Net income of £4.34B (-3.8% Y/Y)
  • Reaffirming guidance for 2024 Based on our current macroeconomic assumptions, for 2024 the Group continues to expect:
  • Banking net interest margin of greater than 290 basis points
  • Operating costs of c.£9.4 billion, including the c.£0.1 billion Bank of England Levy
  • Asset quality ratio to be less than 20 basis points
  • Return on tangible equity of c.13 per cent
  • Capital generation of c.175 basis points2
  • Risk-weighted assets between £220 billion and £225 billion

data.title: TruGolf expects 8-12% topline growth in H2
data.description:
• TruGolf (NASDAQ:TRUG) expects to report strong revenues for H2 2024 driven in part by new revenues from its franchising business, TruGolf Links, as well as by market adoption of new products launched earlier this year.
  • Total sales are expected to grow by 8%-12% by the end of 2024 as compared to 2023 levels.
  • EBITDA for H2 should be greater than $500K showing a return to profitability.
  • "The combination of franchising and growing product sales should lead to strong revenues in the second half of 2024.” said Chris Jones CEO and Director.

data.description:
• Enterprise Bancorp press release (NASDAQ:EBTC): Q3 GAAP EPS of $0.80.
